items.question_text: Under 2-9-1, each ATIS message must be identified by a phonetic letter code word at both the beginning and the _____ of the message.
items.explanation: Per 2-9-1a, identify each ATIS message by a phonetic letter code word at both the beginning and the 'end' of the message. This provides dual-point message identification.

items.question_text: Under 2-9-1, alphabet letter phonetic words are used sequentially from "Alpha" through "_____" and repeated.
items.explanation: Per 2-9-1a1, alphabet letter phonetic words must be used sequentially beginning with 'Alpha' and ending with 'Zulu,' then repeated. The sequence is independent of calendar days.

items.question_text: Under 2-9-2, a new ATIS recording must be made upon receipt of any new official weather regardless of whether there is or is not a change in _____.
items.explanation: Per 2-9-2a1, a new recording must be made upon receipt of any new official weather regardless of whether there is or is not a change in 'values.' This ensures pilots always receive the most current official weather.

items.question_text: Under 2-9-3, ATIS weather observations must always include remarks of lightning, cumulonimbus, and _____ clouds.
items.explanation: Per 2-9-3a4d, ATIS must always include weather observation remarks of lightning, cumulonimbus, and 'towering cumulus' clouds. These are mandatory inclusions regardless of other content.

items.instruction: The current ATIS broadcast reports braking action as 'fair.' A new braking action report is received indicating braking action is now 'poor' on the runway in use.
items.question_text: Under 2-9-2, must a new ATIS recording be made?
items.explanation: Yes. Per 2-9-2a2, a new recording must be made when runway braking action reports indicate runway braking is worse than that included in the current ATIS broadcast. 'Poor' is worse than 'fair.'

choices.text: Yes, because braking action has worsened from the current ATIS broadcast.
No, because braking action is not an official weather observation.
Only if the change occurs within 10 minutes of the last recording.
No, because the ATIS should only be updated at the top of each hour.
